message:
refactor(hook): remove DCG wrapper script, rely on native hook parallelism

Claude Code natively runs PreToolUse hooks in parallel, making the
rch-dcg-chain wrapper script unnecessary. The wrapper added complexity
(~90 lines of Rust + embedded bash) to solve a problem that doesn't
exist: it serialized rch and dcg into a single shell script to avoid
"parallel execution issues", but parallel execution is actually the
correct behavior — rch handles compilation interception while dcg
independently handles destructive command blocking. They don't conflict.

Changes:
- Remove RCH_DCG_WRAPPER_SCRIPT constant (embedded bash script)
- Remove create_hook_command_with_dcg_support() function
- Remove #[cfg(unix)] PermissionsExt import (only used by wrapper chmod)
- Simplify hook_install() to register rch directly without DCG detection
- Change hook insertion from prepend (insert(0, ..)) to append (push(..))
  so rch runs after safety hooks like dcg, not before them
- Replace position()-based detection with simpler any()-based check

The net effect: `rch hook install` now adds a single clean hook entry
pointing directly at the rch binary. If dcg is also installed, Claude
Code runs both hooks in parallel as designed. No wrapper, no coupling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
